questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following represents the structure of neoprene?
questions.solution:
Neoprene is a synthetic rubber formed by the free radical polymerization of chloroprene (2-chloro-1,3-butadiene).

The structure of the monomer chloroprene is $CH_2=C(Cl)-CH=CH_2$.

During polymerization, it undergoes 1,4-addition, shifting the double bond to the center and forming single bonds at the ends to connect with other monomer units.

The polymerization reaction is:
$n \, CH_2=C(Cl)-CH=CH_2 \rightarrow -[-CH_2-C(Cl)=CH-CH_2-]_n-$

This repeating unit matches the structure given in Option (C).

Answer:

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following represents the structure of poly(methyl methacrylate)?
questions.solution:
Poly(methyl methacrylate) is an addition polymer formed from the monomer methyl methacrylate.

The chemical structure of methyl methacrylate is $\text{CH}_2=\text{C}(\text{CH}_3)\text{COOCH}_3$.

During polymerization, the double bond between the carbon atoms opens up to form the polymer backbone, resulting in the repeating unit:
$-[-\text{CH}_2-\text{C}(\text{CH}_3)(\text{COOCH}_3)-]_n-$

Option (D) correctly represents this structure, showing a backbone of $-\text{CH}_2-\text{C}-$ with a methyl group ($-\text{CH}_3$) and an ester group ($-\text{COOCH}_3$) attached to the same carbon atom.

Answer:

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Identify the structure of polyethene.
questions.solution:
Polyethene is an addition polymer formed by the polymerization of ethene ($CH_2=CH_2$) monomers.

During the polymerization process, the double bond between the carbon atoms breaks, and the ethene molecules join together to form a long, continuous chain.

The repeating unit of polyethene is $-[CH_2-CH_2]_n-$.

Looking at the given options, the structure in option (A) correctly represents this repeating unit.

Answer:

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following synthetic polymers is an elastomer that resembles natural rubber?
questions.solution:
Natural rubber is an elastomer. Among the given options, Neoprene is a synthetic polymer that acts as an elastomer and resembles natural rubber in its properties.

Neoprene is a polymer of chloroprene (2-chloro-1,3-butadiene).

Nylon is a polyamide fibre, and Glyptal is a polyester resin. Chloroprene is a monomer, not a polymer.

Answer: Neoprene

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following polymers is widely used for lubrication and as an electrical insulator?
questions.solution:
Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE), commonly known as Teflon, is a synthetic fluoropolymer of tetrafluoroethylene.

It has a very low coefficient of friction, making it highly effective as a solid lubricant. It is also chemically inert and possesses excellent dielectric properties, which makes it widely used as an electrical insulator in cables and connector assemblies.

PVC is used for pipes and general insulation but not for lubrication. SBR is a synthetic rubber used primarily in tires. PAN is used to make synthetic fibers like Orlon.

Answer: PTFE

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Among cellulose, polyvinyl chloride, nylon, and natural rubber, the polymer in which the intermolecular forces of attraction are the weakest is:
questions.solution:
Polymers are classified based on the magnitude of their intermolecular forces into elastomers, thermoplastics, fibers, and thermosetting polymers.

Elastomers are polymers in which the polymer chains are held together by the weakest intermolecular forces. These weak binding forces permit the polymer to be stretched. Natural rubber is an example of an elastomer.

Fibers are thread-forming solids which possess high tensile strength and high modulus. These characteristics are due to strong intermolecular forces like hydrogen bonding. Nylon and cellulose belong to this category.

Thermoplastic polymers possess intermolecular forces of attraction intermediate between elastomers and fibers. Polyvinyl chloride is a thermoplastic polymer.

Therefore, natural rubber has the weakest intermolecular forces of attraction among the given options.

Answer: Natural rubber

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following represents the structure of poly(methyl acrylate)?
questions.solution:
The monomer of poly(methyl acrylate) is methyl acrylate.

The chemical formula of methyl acrylate is $\text{CH}_2=\text{CH}-\text{COOCH}_3$.

During addition polymerization, the double bond between the carbon atoms breaks to form single bonds with adjacent monomers, leading to the formation of a long polymer chain.

The polymerization reaction can be represented as:
$n \text{CH}_2=\text{CH}-\text{COOCH}_3 \xrightarrow{\text{Polymerization}} -[\text{CH}_2-\text{CH}(\text{COOCH}_3)]_n-$

This repeating unit matches the structure given in option (A).

Answer:

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Nylon threads are made of:
questions.solution:
Nylon is a synthetic polymer that belongs to the class of polyamides.

It is formed by the condensation polymerization of a diamine and a dicarboxylic acid (for example, Nylon-6,6 is synthesized from hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid) or by the ring-opening polymerization of a lactam (for example, Nylon-6 is synthesized from caprolactam).

The repeating units in the polymer chain are linked together by amide bonds ($-CO-NH-$).

Therefore, nylon threads are made of polyamide polymer.

Answer: polyamide polymer

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Which of the following polymers are both addition polymers and homopolymers?
I. Neoprene
II. Polystyrene
III. Nylon-6,6
IV. Nylon-6
questions.solution:
Neoprene is synthesized by the addition polymerization of chloroprene (2-chloro-1,3-butadiene). Since it is formed from a single type of monomer, it is both an addition polymer and a homopolymer.

Polystyrene is synthesized by the addition polymerization of styrene. It is also formed from a single type of monomer, making it both an addition polymer and a homopolymer.

Nylon-6,6 is synthesized by the condensation polymerization of hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid. It is a condensation polymer and a copolymer.

Nylon-6 is synthesized by the condensation polymerization of caprolactam. Although it is a homopolymer, it is a condensation polymer, not an addition polymer.

Therefore, only Neoprene (I) and Polystyrene (II) are both addition polymers and homopolymers.

Answer: I and II

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is NOT considered a biopolymer?
questions.solution:
Biopolymers are polymers produced by living organisms that are essential for life processes and are typically made of biomolecules like amino acids, sugars, or nucleotides. Examples include proteins (polymers of amino acids), nucleic acids (polymers of nucleotides), and starch or cellulose (polymers of carbohydrates).

Rubber is a natural polymer (polyisoprene) obtained from the latex of rubber trees, but it is considered a secondary metabolite rather than a biopolymer, as it is not involved in the primary structural or functional processes of the organism in the same way as proteins, nucleic acids, or polysaccharides.

Answer: Rubber

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is NOT an example of a natural polymer?
questions.solution:
Natural polymers are those that are found in nature, typically in plants and animals. Examples include proteins, cellulose, starch, resins, etc.

Silk and wool are natural protein fibers.

Leather is derived from animal skin, which is primarily composed of collagen, a natural protein polymer.

Nylon is a synthetic polymer (a polyamide) manufactured through chemical processes.

Therefore, Nylon is not a natural polymer.

Answer: Nylon

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following monomers is used to prepare the polymer polyvinyl chloride (PVC)?
questions.solution:
Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) is an addition polymer.

It is prepared by the polymerization of the monomer vinyl chloride.

The chemical formula of vinyl chloride is $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CHCl}$.

Answer: $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CHCl}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Nylon belongs to which of the following classes of polymers?
questions.solution:
Nylon is a synthetic polymer that contains amide linkages ($-CO-NH-$) in its main chain.

It is formed by the condensation polymerization of a diamine and a dicarboxylic acid (for example, Nylon 6,6 is made from hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid) or by the ring-opening polymerization of a lactam (for example, Nylon 6 is made from caprolactam).

Because of the presence of these amide linkages, Nylon is classified as a polyamide fibre.

Answer: Polyamide fibre

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Among the following polymers, identify the copolymer.
questions.solution:
Starch is a naturally occurring homopolymer made up of only one type of monomer, $\alpha$-D-glucose.

Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) is an addition homopolymer formed by the polymerization of a single monomer, vinyl chloride.

Nylon-6 is a condensation homopolymer formed by the polymerization of a single monomer, caprolactam.

Proteins are naturally occurring condensation polymers formed by the linkage of various different $\alpha$-amino acids through peptide bonds. Since they are derived from more than one type of monomer, proteins are copolymers (heteropolymers).

Answer: Protein

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Polythene is a polymer obtained by the polymerisation of:
questions.solution:
Polythene (polyethylene) is an addition polymer formed by the repeated addition of ethylene (ethene) monomer molecules.

The polymerisation reaction is:
$n \text{CH}_2=\text{CH}_2 \xrightarrow{\text{Polymerisation}} -[\text{CH}_2-\text{CH}_2]_n-$

Styrene is the monomer for polystyrene, butadiene for polybutadiene, and isoprene for natural rubber (polyisoprene).

Answer: Ethylene

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following pairs of monomers is used in the preparation of nylon-6,6?
questions.solution:
Nylon-6,6 is a condensation polymer formed by the copolymerization of adipic acid and hexamethylenediamine.

Adipic acid has the chemical formula $HOOC-(CH_2)_4-COOH$, which contains 6 carbon atoms.

Hexamethylenediamine has the chemical formula $H_2N-(CH_2)_6-NH_2$, which also contains 6 carbon atoms.

The "6,6" in nylon-6,6 indicates that both monomers consist of 6 carbon atoms each.

Therefore, the correct pair of monomers is adipic acid and hexamethylenediamine.

Answer: Adipic acid and hexamethylenediamine

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A polyamide synthetic polymer prepared by the prolonged heating of caprolactam is:
questions.solution:
Caprolactam is a cyclic amide with six carbon atoms. When it is heated with water at high temperatures, the ring opens up to form $\epsilon$-aminocaproic acid, which then undergoes polymerization to yield the polyamide known as nylon-6.

Nylon-6, 6 is prepared from hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid.

Nylon-6, 10 is prepared from hexamethylenediamine and sebacic acid.

Glyptal is a polyester prepared from ethylene glycol and phthalic acid.

Answer: nylon-6

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following represents the structure of neoprene?
questions.solution:
Neoprene is a synthetic rubber formed by the free radical polymerization of chloroprene (2-chloro-1,3-butadiene).

The structure of the monomer chloroprene is:
$\text{CH}_2=\text{C}(\text{Cl})-\text{CH}=\text{CH}_2$

During polymerization, it undergoes 1,4-addition to form the repeating unit of neoprene:
$n \, \text{CH}_2=\text{C}(\text{Cl})-\text{CH}=\text{CH}_2 \xrightarrow{\text{Polymerization}} -[\text{CH}_2-\text{C}(\text{Cl})=\text{CH}-\text{CH}_2]_n-$

This structure matches the one given in option (C).

Answer:

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Buna-N synthetic rubber is a copolymer of:
questions.solution:
Buna-N is a synthetic rubber obtained by the copolymerization of 1,3-butadiene and acrylonitrile in the presence of a peroxide catalyst.

The chemical formula for 1,3-butadiene is $\mathrm{H_2C=CH-CH=CH_2}$.
The chemical formula for acrylonitrile is $\mathrm{H_2C=CH-CN}$.

Therefore, Buna-N is a copolymer of $\mathrm{H_2C=CH-CN}$ and $\mathrm{H_2C=CH-CH=CH_2}$.

Answer: $\mathrm{H}_{2}\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{CN}$ and $\mathrm{H}_{2}\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CH}-\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}_{2}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is a chain-growth polymer?
questions.solution:
Chain-growth polymers (also known as addition polymers) are formed by the repeated addition of monomer molecules possessing double or triple bonds without the elimination of any small molecules.

Polypropylene is formed by the addition polymerization of propylene ($CH_3-CH=CH_2$). Therefore, it is a chain-growth polymer.

Nylon-6, Nylon-6,6, and Glyptal are formed by condensation polymerization (step-growth polymerization) which involves the elimination of small molecules such as water.

Answer: Polypropylene

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following fibres is NOT made up of polyamides?
questions.solution:
Nylon is a synthetic polyamide fibre.

Natural silk and wool are natural protein fibres. Proteins are polymers of amino acids linked by peptide bonds, which are essentially amide linkages. Thus, they are natural polyamides.

Artificial silk, also known as rayon, is a regenerated cellulose fibre. Cellulose is a polysaccharide, not a polyamide.

Therefore, artificial silk is not made up of polyamides.

Answer: Artificial silk

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Dacron is an example of:
questions.solution:
Dacron, also known as polyethylene terephthalate (PET), is formed by the condensation polymerization of ethylene glycol and terephthalic acid.

The reaction involves the elimination of water molecules, resulting in the formation of ester linkages between the monomer units.

Since the polymer chain consists of repeating ester linkages, Dacron is classified as a polyester.

Answer: polyester

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The process of heating natural rubber with sulphur to improve its physical properties is known as
questions.solution:
Natural rubber is soft and sticky at high temperatures and brittle at low temperatures. To improve its physical properties, it is heated with sulphur and an appropriate additive at a temperature range between $373\text{ K}$ to $415\text{ K}$.

This process of heating natural rubber with sulphur is known as vulcanisation.

During vulcanisation, sulphur forms cross-links at the reactive sites of double bonds, making the rubber stiff and strong.

Answer: vulcanisation

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The monomer unit of the polymer Orlon is:
questions.solution:
Orlon is the commercial name for polyacrylonitrile (PAN).

It is an addition polymer formed by the polymerization of acrylonitrile in the presence of a peroxide catalyst.

The common name for acrylonitrile is vinyl cyanide, and its chemical formula is $CH_2=CH-CN$.

The polymerization reaction is:
$n CH_2=CH-CN \rightarrow [-CH_2-CH(CN)-]_n$

Therefore, the monomer unit of Orlon is vinyl cyanide.

Answer: vinyl cyanide

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which one of the following statements is incorrect?
options: PTFE stands for polytetrafluoroethylene, commonly known as Teflon.
questions.solution:
PVC stands for polyvinyl chloride, which is a widely used synthetic plastic polymer.

Buna-S is a synthetic rubber, which is a copolymer of 1,3-butadiene and styrene. Natural rubber is a polymer of isoprene (2-methyl-1,3-butadiene). Thus, Buna-S does not stand for natural rubber.

PMMA stands for polymethyl methacrylate, a transparent thermoplastic often used as a lightweight or shatter-resistant alternative to glass.

PTFE stands for polytetrafluoroethylene, which is a synthetic fluoropolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, commonly known by the brand name Teflon.

Therefore, the statement that Buna-S stands for natural rubber is incorrect.

Answer: Buna-S stands for natural rubber.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Bakelite is a polymer obtained by the reaction of phenol with:
questions.solution:
Bakelite is a condensation polymer formed by the reaction of phenol with formaldehyde in the presence of either an acid or a base catalyst.

The initial reaction involves the formation of ortho and/or para hydroxymethylphenol derivatives, which further react with phenol to form linear polymers called novolac. Upon further heating with formaldehyde, novolac undergoes cross-linking to form the infusible solid mass known as Bakelite.

Answer: Formaldehyde

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following sets consists entirely of addition polymers?
questions.solution:
Addition polymers are formed by the repeated addition of monomer molecules possessing double or triple bonds without the elimination of any small molecules.

Polyethylene is formed by the addition polymerization of ethene.

PVC (Polyvinyl chloride) is formed by the addition polymerization of vinyl chloride.

Acrilan (Polyacrylonitrile) is formed by the addition polymerization of acrylonitrile.

Bakelite, terylene, and nylon are condensation polymers formed by the elimination of small molecules like water.

Answer: Polyethylene, PVC, Acrilan

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is a raw material used in the manufacture of nylon?
questions.solution:
Nylon-6,6 is a widely used polyamide prepared by the condensation polymerization of two monomers: hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid.

Thus, adipic acid is a basic raw material used in the manufacture of nylon.

Among the other options, butadiene and isoprene are monomers used in the manufacture of synthetic and natural rubbers respectively, while ethylene is used to manufacture polyethylene.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following polymers is obtained by condensation polymerisation?
questions.solution:
Teflon is formed by the addition polymerisation of tetrafluoroethene.

Polythene is formed by the addition polymerisation of ethene.

PVC is formed by the addition polymerisation of vinyl chloride.

Phenol-formaldehyde resin (such as Bakelite or Novolac) is obtained by the condensation polymerisation of phenol and formaldehyde, which involves the elimination of water molecules.

Answer: Phenol-formaldehyde resin

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Terylene is a condensation polymer formed from ethylene glycol and which of the following compounds?
questions.solution:
Terylene, also known as Dacron, is a polyester.

It is prepared by the condensation polymerization of ethylene glycol (ethane-1,2-diol) and terephthalic acid (benzene-1,4-dicarboxylic acid) at $420$ to $460$ K in the presence of a catalyst such as zinc acetate-antimony trioxide.

The reaction involves the elimination of water molecules to form ester linkages.

Answer: Terephthalic acid

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Identify the monomer used in the preparation of the polymer Teflon.
questions.solution:
Teflon is the commercial name for polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE).

It is an addition polymer formed by the polymerization of tetrafluoroethylene.

The chemical reaction for its preparation is:
$n \mathrm{CF}_{2}=\mathrm{CF}_{2} \xrightarrow{\text{Catalyst/High pressure}} [-\mathrm{CF}_{2}-\mathrm{CF}_{2}-]_n$

Thus, the monomer used in the preparation of Teflon is tetrafluoroethylene, which is $\mathrm{F}_{2} \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CF}_{2}$.

Answer: $\mathrm{F}_{2} \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CF}_{2}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The widely used plastic PVC (polyvinyl chloride) is the polymerisation product of:
questions.solution:
Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) is an addition polymer formed by the polymerisation of vinyl chloride.

The chemical name of vinyl chloride is chloroethene, and its structure is $\mathrm{H}_{2}\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CHCl}$.

The polymerisation reaction is:
$n \mathrm{H}_{2}\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CHCl} \xrightarrow{\text{Polymerisation}} -[\mathrm{CH}_{2}-\mathrm{CHCl}]_{n}-$

Answer: $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{CHCl}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following polymers is formed by an addition polymerisation reaction?
questions.solution:
Addition polymerisation involves the repeated addition of monomer molecules possessing double or triple bonds to form a polymer, without the elimination of any small molecules.

Polythene is formed by the addition polymerisation of ethene ($CH_2=CH_2$).

Terylene is a polyester formed by the condensation polymerisation of ethylene glycol and terephthalic acid.

Nylon is a polyamide formed by the condensation polymerisation of hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid (in the case of Nylon-6,6).

Glyptal is a polyester formed by the condensation polymerisation of ethylene glycol and phthalic acid.

Therefore, Polythene is the only polymer formed by an addition polymerisation reaction.

Answer: Polythene

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The polymer fibre obtained by the condensation of hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid is:
questions.solution:
The condensation polymerization of hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid produces Nylon-6,6.

Hexamethylenediamine has 6 carbon atoms and adipic acid also has 6 carbon atoms, which gives the polymer its name Nylon-6,6.

The reaction is:
$n \text{H}_2\text{N}-(\text{CH}_2)_6-\text{NH}_2 + n \text{HOOC}-(\text{CH}_2)_4-\text{COOH} \rightarrow [-\text{NH}-(\text{CH}_2)_6-\text{NH}-\text{CO}-(\text{CH}_2)_4-\text{CO}-]_n + 2n \text{H}_2\text{O}$

Answer: Nylon-6,6

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is a naturally occurring polymer?
questions.solution:
Polythene is a synthetic polymer made from the polymerization of ethene.

Buna-S is a synthetic rubber formed by the copolymerization of 1,3-butadiene and styrene.

Bakelite is a synthetic polymer formed by the condensation reaction of phenol with formaldehyde.

Proteins are naturally occurring polymers made up of amino acid monomers linked by peptide bonds.

Answer: Protein

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is a thermosetting polymer?
questions.solution:
Thermosetting polymers are cross-linked or heavily branched molecules, which on heating undergo extensive cross-linking in moulds and again become infusible. They cannot be reused.

Bakelite is a phenol-formaldehyde resin and is a well-known example of a thermosetting polymer.

Nylon-6 and Nylon-6,6 are polyamides which act as fibres and are thermoplastic in nature.

SBR (Styrene-Butadiene Rubber) is an elastomer.

Answer: Bakelite

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Natural rubber is a polymer of:
questions.solution:
Natural rubber is a naturally occurring polymer obtained from the latex of rubber trees.

It is a linear polymer of isoprene, which is chemically known as 2-methyl-1,3-butadiene.

The polymer is also referred to as cis-1,4-polyisoprene.

Answer: isoprene

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Isoprene is the monomer used in the production of:
questions.solution:
Isoprene (2-methyl-1,3-butadiene) is the monomeric unit of natural rubber.

Natural rubber is a linear polymer of isoprene, specifically cis-1,4-polyisoprene.

Answer: natural rubber

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following fibres is a polyamide?
questions.solution:
Nylon is a synthetic polymer that falls under the category of polyamides. It is formed by the condensation polymerization of a diamine and a dicarboxylic acid (e.g., Nylon-6,6 is formed from hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid) or by the ring-opening polymerization of a lactam (e.g., Nylon-6 from caprolactam).

Rayon is a regenerated cellulose fibre.

Dacron (PET) is a polyester.

Orlon is a polyacrylonitrile (PAN) fibre.

Answer: Nylon

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following is the monomer of neoprene?
questions.solution:
Neoprene is a synthetic rubber produced by the free radical polymerization of chloroprene.

The chemical name of chloroprene is 2-chloro-1,3-butadiene.

Isoprene is the monomer of natural rubber.

Butadiene is used in the preparation of Buna-S and Buna-N.

Styrene is used in the preparation of polystyrene and Buna-S.

Therefore, the monomer of neoprene is chloroprene.

Answer: Chloroprene
